Moreover, the proposal is not symmetric — I would be giving up more in quantity and potentially in value. Since I have 25 X, I can afford to lose 10, but I only have 5 Y, so gaining 5 more Y is good, but the cost in X is high.

- I gain 8 Y (more than the 5 they offered to give)
- The effective ratio is 5 X for 8 Y → 0.625 X per Y, which is better than the 2 X per Y in their proposal.

This makes the trade more appealing to me, and since I still have 2 proposals left, I can afford to be strategic.

I’ll go with this counterproposal.
